novel_features.name: Trend digest
novel_features.description: Generate a week-over-week digest of new vs repeat trends in a category with top rising keywords.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local first_seen timestamp on every trend - the website has no week view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents pulling a weekly competitive scan can ask 'what's new in eco this week' without re-clicking 30 cards by hand.

novel_features.name: Category watch
novel_features.description: Per-category new-only feed - fills the gap that TrendHunter's RSS is global-only.
novel_features.rationale: Synthesizes a category-scoped feed from synced category pages plus local dedup; per-category RSS upstream returns an empty channel.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents and indie scouts get a clean delta for one vertical without re-processing the global firehose.

novel_features.name: FAQ extraction
novel_features.description: Extract the FAQPage JSON-LD from a trend page into structured Q&A.
novel_features.rationale: TrendHunter embeds FAQPage Q&A on every /trends/<slug> page. No prior tool reads it; it's the highest-density agent-ready summary on the site.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents get a ready-to-quote Q&A summary of each trend without prompting another LLM to summarize the article.

novel_features.name: Rising clusters
novel_features.description: Surface rising keyword clusters via FTS5 co-occurrence with a rising-vs-prior-window delta.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local-corpus analytics - requires an FTS5 keywords + body_text index and joined first_seen windows that don't exist anywhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents researching an emerging theme see which adjacent keywords are accelerating, not just the headline trends.

novel_features.name: Author velocity
novel_features.description: Rank TrendHunter authors and futurists by time-windowed publish rate, not lifetime count.
novel_features.rationale: The site's /scoreboard tracks lifetime metrics only. With local first_seen per trend we can window the rate to any horizon.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Find which trend hunters are currently active in your space, not just who has the biggest historical footprint.

novel_features.name: Megatrend map
novel_features.description: Join the megatrend index, the related-trend graph, and keyword overlap into one view that maps free trends to a paid megatrend bucket.
novel_features.rationale: Combines three site surfaces (megatrends, related slugs, FAQ keywords) that the site itself never presents together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Evaluators deciding whether a paid megatrend report is worth $295 can see which free trends ladder up to it.

novel_features.name: Agent brief
novel_features.description: One-shot agent brief: top-N ranked trends in a category, each with FAQ Q&A and keywords, rendered as JSON or markdown.
novel_features.rationale: Bundles ranked corpus selection with FAQ extraction and a structured envelope tuned for LLM ingestion - no other tool does both halves.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Plugs straight into an agent prompt as ground truth for a market memo, no glue code needed.

novel_features.name: Inbox cursor
novel_features.description: Show trends seen since the last invocation of `inbox` - a per-machine cursor table.
novel_features.rationale: Stateful local feature that the website cannot offer because the cursor lives on the user's machine.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Build it into a Monday-morning routine and only ever see things you haven't seen before.

novel_features.name: Business scout
novel_features.description: Pull top trends in a category, score each by relevance to a business profile, optionally route the scoring through a local LLM.
novel_features.rationale: Combines local corpus ranking with optional LLM scoring and emits a pipe-friendly shape designed for handoff to downstream research tools (like /last30days). No existing tool composes these three layers.
novel_features.example: trendhunter-pp-cli scout --category kitchen --business "We sell smart ovens for home cooks" --top 10 --llm
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents researching a vertical get a ranked, business-scoped trend list ready to pipe into the next stage of their pipeline.

narrative.headline: Every Trend Hunter feature, plus a local SQLite corpus, FTS5 search, FAQ Q&A extraction, and agent-native JSON no other Trend Hunter tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: trendhunter-pp-cli scrapes the free public TrendHunter surface (RSS feed, sitemap, category and trend pages, search results) into a local SQLite store, then exposes corpus-diff, rising-keyword clusters, time-windowed author velocity, FAQ Q&A extraction, and a category-watch feed the site itself can't provide. No API key. No login. Stdlib HTTP transport - no headless browser.

quickstart.comment: Pull the latest 30 trends + sitemap and write them to the local parsed store.
Search the local corpus first, with a flag to fan-out live.
Week-over-week digest with new vs repeat split.
Pull the FAQ JSON-LD from any /trends/<slug> page.
One-shot agent brief: ranked trends with FAQ Q&A.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns no results after install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `trendhunter-pp-cli pull` first - search is local-first and needs a corpus.
Update the CLI; the default Chrome UA may be too old for the site's filter. `go install ...@latest` and try again.

troubleshoots.symptom: Trend page redirects to a vertical microsite (cleanthesky.com etc)
troubleshoots.fix: The CLI follows redirects; the canonical /trends/<slug> URL still resolves and is what the local store records.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you want TrendHunter data inside an agent loop, a script, or a daily routine - rather than clicking the site by hand. It's strongest for week-over-week trend monitoring, FAQ-style trend summaries an LLM can quote, and corpus-wide keyword clustering. It's a free, no-auth scraper; for the official paid API surface, use TrendHunter's commercial offering instead.

recipes.title: Weekly trend memo for eco
recipes.explanation: Returns just the title, keywords, and first-seen timestamp for every new eco trend in the past week. Narrow surface; great for a slack post or LLM prompt.

recipes.title: Agent brief for AI
recipes.explanation: Top 10 AI trends, each with FAQ Q&A and keywords, rendered as markdown ready to paste into a prompt.

recipes.title: Rising-keyword scan
recipes.explanation: Surfaces keywords whose co-occurrence count rose vs the prior 30-day window.

recipes.title: Active futurists this month
recipes.explanation: Time-windowed publish velocity for trend hunters and futurists - the site's /scoreboard is lifetime-only.

recipes.title: Map a megatrend to free trends
recipes.explanation: Joins the megatrend index, the related-trend graph, and keyword overlap to show which free trends ladder up to one megatrend bucket.
